name: ThousandEyes API
what_it_does: Enterprise internet and network intelligence platform (Cisco-acquired) with REST API for network path analysis, BGP monitoring, cloud and SaaS performance monitoring, and internet outage detection from thousands of global vantage points.
use_cases: Querying network path analysis and packet loss data for root cause determination agents
Monitoring SaaS application performance (Office 365, Salesforce, AWS) from enterprise locations
BGP routing anomaly detection and ISP outage correlation for network NOC automation
Creating and managing synthetic tests (HTTP, DNS, BGP) programmatically
Correlating network intelligence data with internal observability for incident response
not_for: Infrastructure monitoring within private networks (use Prometheus/Zabbix instead)
Small businesses — ThousandEyes targets enterprise and carrier networks
Simple website uptime monitoring (use Pingdom or UptimeRobot)
best_when: An agent needs enterprise-grade internet and network path visibility to diagnose whether performance issues are ISP, CDN, cloud provider, or internal network related.
avoid_when: You need monitoring within your own data center or VPC — ThousandEyes monitors from external vantage points.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with Cisco SSO for v7 API. Legacy basic auth with email + API token for older endpoints. Bearer token auth recommended for new integrations. Scopes control access to test management, agents, and data.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; trial available via Cisco
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing: typically $50k-$500k+/year depending on agent count and features
pricing.notes: ThousandEyes is Cisco enterprise product — contact sales. Pricing based on endpoint agents, cloud agent units, and tests. Significant investment for enterprise network teams.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Comprehensive OpenAPI v7 documentation via Cisco DevNet. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. Rich data model for network paths, BGP routes, and test results. Webhook delivery for alert events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Test creation by name can produce duplicates — check before creating. Alert rules and integrations updates are idempotent by ID.
known_agent_gotchas: v7 API (OAuth2) and v6/legacy (basic auth) coexist — check which version covers needed endpoints
Test result data is time-series — agents must specify start/end time windows for queries
Agent health affects test reliability — agents must be online and healthy for valid data
BGP monitoring requires specific subscription — not included in all ThousandEyes plans

security_score_components.security_notes: Cisco enterprise security standards. FedRAMP authorized. SOC2 Type II and ISO 27001. OAuth2 with Cisco SSO. Data handling under Cisco's enterprise security program.
